trait.name: Unusual Nature
entries: The Weaver does not require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The Weaver can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Cocoon of Fate
entries: While a rider is within the Weaver's structure, they are firmly held by magical threads. The rider has advantage on Dexterity saving throws and checks made to avoid being dismounted.

action.name: Needle of Fate
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}1 ({@damage 1}) piercing damage, plus 6 ({@damage 1d8 + 1}) psychic damage.

action.name: Fate Cocoon
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one Medium or smaller creature. {@h} The target is {@condition restrained} and pulled inside the cocoon. While inside, the target gains the benefits of the Cocoon of Fate as described in the Traits section. The Weaver can hold only one creature (willing or unwilling) inside the cocoon at a time.

Shadow Weavers are eerie, alien entities born of the Umbra, inhabiting the darkest corners of Ethion. Though their nature is diabolical, they lack a conventional moral compass or personal ambition. They have no alignment other than absolute devotion to their task: capturing, imprisoning, and transporting souls or living beings for their masters.

entries.name: Appearance and Physiology
entries: A Shadow Weaver is a complex organism composed of hundreds of sharp, obsidian-black needles and rods held together by viscous violet threads that pulse like veins. These so-called "threads of fate" are magical fibers the Weaver spins from the very essence of suffering and inevitability.
It lacks a distinct head; instead, a crown of glowing, cold crystalline shards floats above its body, where the Weaver stores the energy of previously consumed souls.

At the center of its body lies a gaping, pulsing void emitting the same hue as the threads it spins. If it chooses, the Weaver can expand its ribcage, enlarging the void enough to fit a Medium-sized creature inside. The obsidian rods then form a sturdy frame resembling a bizarre cage.

entries.name: Hunting Tactics
entries: Shadow Weavers are perfect hunters. They prefer to lurk in the thick shadows of dungeons, crypts, or shadow pockets. Their primary method of attack is a sudden jab from one of their sharp needles, dealing an onset of existential dread rather than physical pain.

Once the target is disoriented, the Weaver instantly enwraps them in its threads of fate, placing them in a cocoon held within the central void of its body. While the Weaver carries its victim, it telepathically whispers about the futility of resistance to break their will.

entries.name: Use as a Mount
entries: Due to their high speed and excellent mobility, Shadow Weavers are sometimes used by their masters as mounts. In such cases, the Cocoon of Fate does not fully entangle the rider but merely holds them inside without restricting their movement.
